core_mechanism: spectral zeta-function regularization: assigning a finite value to a divergent sum over the discrete eigenvalue spectrum of a boundary-value problem by analytically continuing the associated zeta function, then differentiating it to extract a finite observable
brief_reason: The abstract states the vacuum (Casimir) energy and pressure are obtained via the zeta function technique from the discrete mode spectrum fixed by boundary conditions and compactification, i.e. analytic continuation of a spectral zeta function to render a divergent mode sum finite.
